image.alt: Loosely painted, unfinished self-portrait of an older man in a dark cap against a dark background

attribution.fileTitle: File:Unfinished Self-Portrait, Rembrandt van Rijn, c. 1659, Musée Granet, Aix-en-Provence.jpg
attribution.author: Rembrandt

rounds.revealFact: Rembrandt van Rijn, the Dutch master of light and shadow, painted himself dozens of times across his life, leaving one of art's great visual diaries.

image.alt: Painting of a chaotic last stand as cavalrymen with revolvers are overwhelmed by mounted warriors on open grassland

attribution.fileTitle: File:Edgar Samuel Paxson - Custer's Last Stand.jpg
attribution.author: Edgar Samuel Paxson

clues: A confident cavalry commander split his regiment and attacked a huge encampment, badly underestimating the warriors defending it.
His entire detachment was surrounded and wiped out, a stunning defeat that shocked a nation.

pairs.a: Mercator devises his famous map projection for sailors
pairs.b: Ptolemy compiles his great atlas of the known world

pairs.a: Abel Tasman becomes the first European to reach New Zealand
pairs.b: Vitus Bering charts the strait between Asia and America

pairs.a: David Livingstone traces the course of the Zambezi in Africa
pairs.b: Lewis and Clark map a route to the Pacific across North America

pairs.a: Roald Amundsen's team is first to reach the South Pole
pairs.b: Robert Peary claims to reach the North Pole

pairs.a: John Harrison's marine chronometer finally solves the longitude problem
pairs.b: The first solo sailing voyage around the world is completed

image.alt: Black-and-white photo of rows of men seated in a wooden dock flanked by white-helmeted guards in a courtroom

attribution.fileTitle: File:Defendants in the dock at the Nuremberg Trials.jpg
attribution.author: U.S. Army Signal Corps

followUpQuestion.prompt: These trials prosecuted the leaders of which regime?

rounds.prompt: Howard Carter opens the long-sealed tomb of the boy pharaoh Tutankhamun.
rounds.revealFact: The near-intact tomb of Tutankhamun, found in 1922, filled museums with golden treasures and sparked a worldwide craze for ancient Egypt.

rounds.prompt: Where did a great empire raise a ceremonial capital of vast columned halls and grand stairways, later burned to the ground by a conqueror?
rounds.revealFact: Persepolis was the ceremonial capital of the Persian Achaemenid Empire until Alexander the Great's army burned it in 330 BCE.
